text: What if I told you your cells have a ticking clock? Meet telomeres — the protective caps at the ends of your chromosomes. Every time a cell divides, these caps wear down, shortening your lifespan. Telomerase is an enzyme that can rebuild these caps, but it’s not a cure-all. Understanding telomeres gives us a glimpse into the biology of aging. Curious about how this affects you? Like and follow for more insights!
